steps.title: Find the sample proportion
steps.content: In the sample, $4$ of $20$ lawns contain fertilizer: $$\frac{4}{20}=0.2$$

steps.title: Scale to the town
steps.content: Apply the same proportion to $260$ lawns: $$0.2\cdot260=52$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice D is $52$, so D is correct.

steps.title: Check the estimate against the choices
steps.content: Since $4$ out of $20$ is one-fifth of the sample, the estimate should be one-fifth of $260$. One-fifth of $260$ is $52$, not a very small number like $3$, $5$, or $13$.
